description: Deep research profile. Uses citation footnotes (e.g., [1], [2]) with sources listed at document end. Users can assess reliability themselves. No hard threshold on groundedness. Suited for literature reviews, comprehensive surveys, and multi-source report generation.

bootstrap.description: Read the saga task.json so the worker knows the research goal and current stage.
Read progress.json to see which sources and claims are already verified.
Read the most recent checkpoint to resume context and source list.

hard.title: Citations present
hard.passDescription: citations exist and link to identifiable sources
hard.failReworkDescription: citations are missing but sources clearly exist to support the claims
hard.failEscalateDescription: the claims are about proprietary/internal information that cannot be publicly cited

hard.title: Completeness
hard.failReworkDescription: goal is partially addressed; more work would help
hard.failEscalateDescription: the goal asks for information not publicly available

hard.title: Factual specificity
hard.failReworkDescription: mostly generic statements; more research can fix
hard.failEscalateDescription: specifics genuinely do not exist in public domain

soft.title: Source diversity
soft.scoringGuide: 5 = diverse primary + secondary; 1 = single source repeated

soft.title: Analytical depth
soft.scoringGuide: 5 = synthesis with identified tensions; 1 = list of facts

soft.title: Actionable conclusions
soft.scoringGuide: 5 = specific action items with rationale; 1 = description only

criteria.description: All declared research questions and done-criteria are addressed with evidence.
criteria.scoringGuide: 5 = every question answered with direct evidence; 1 = most questions unanswered.

criteria.description: Factual claims use citation footnotes (e.g., [1], [2]) with sources listed in 'References' section.
criteria.scoringGuide: 5 = all key claims have footnote citations or [citation needed] markers; 1 = significant claims without any citation mechanism.

criteria.description: Multiple independent sources consulted; not over-reliant on a single source.
criteria.scoringGuide: 5 = diverse primary and secondary sources; 1 = single source used throughout.

criteria.description: Analysis goes beyond summary; synthesises across sources and identifies tensions.
criteria.scoringGuide: 5 = genuine synthesis with identified contradictions; 1 = raw summaries only.

criteria.description: Report is structured, readable, and a successor worker could resume from it.
criteria.scoringGuide: 5 = self-contained, well-structured; 1 = requires original chat to interpret.

benchGoal.goal: Summarise the key arguments for and against static typing in programming languages and write a brief report to report.md.
